verses.latin_vulgate: Et ascendens in naviculam, transfretavit, et venit in civitatem suam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐμβὰς εἰς τὸ πλοῖον διεπέρασεν καὶ ἦλθεν εἰς τὴν ἰδίαν πόλιν
verses.king_james: And he entered into a ship, and passed over, and came into his own city.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ecce offerebant ei paralyticum jacentem in lecto. Et videns Jesus fidem illorum, dixit paralytico: Confide fili, remittuntur tibi peccata tua.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἰδού προσέφερον αὐτῷ παραλυτικὸν ἐπὶ κλίνης βεβλημένον καὶ ἰδὼν ὁ ἰησοῦς τὴν πίστιν αὐτῶν εἶπεν τῷ παραλυτικῷ φάρσει τέκνον ἀφέωνταί σοί αἱ ἁμαρτίαι σου
verses.king_james: And, behold, they brought to him a man sick of the palsy, lying on a bed: and Jesus seeing their faith said unto the sick of the palsy; Son, be of good cheer; thy sins be forgiven thee.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ecce quidam de scribis dixerunt intra se: Hic blasphemat.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἰδού τινες τῶν γραμματέων εἶπον ἐν ἑαυτοῖς οὗτος βλασφημεῖ
verses.king_james: And, behold, certain of the scribes said within themselves, This *man* blasphemeth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cum vidisset Jesus cogitationes eorum, dixit: Ut quid cogitatis mala in cordibus vestris?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἰδὼν ὁ ἰησοῦς τὰς ἐνθυμήσεις αὐτῶν εἶπεν ἵνα τί ὑμεῖς ἐνθυμεῖσθε πονηρὰ ἐν ταῖς καρδίαις ὑμῶν
verses.king_james: And Jesus knowing their thoughts said, Wherefore think ye evil in your hearts?

verses.latin_vulgate: Quid est facilius dicere: Dimittuntur tibi peccata tua: an dicere: Surge, et ambula?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τί γάρ ἐστιν εὐκοπώτερον εἰπεῖν ἀφέωνταί σοι αἱ ἁμαρτίαι ἢ εἰπεῖν ἕγειραι καὶ περιπάτει
verses.king_james: For whether is easier, to say, *Thy* sins be forgiven thee; or to say, Arise, and walk?

verses.latin_vulgate: Ut autem sciatis, quia Filius hominis habet potestatem in terra dimittendi peccata, tunc ait paralytico: Surge, tolle lectum tuum, et vade in domum tuam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἵνα δὲ εἰδῆτε ὅτι ἐξουσίαν ἔχει ὁ υἱὸς τοῦ ἀνθρώπου ἐπὶ τῆς γῆς ἀφιέναι ἁμαρτίας τότε λέγει τῷ παραλυτικῷ ἐγερθεὶς ἆρόν σου τὴν κλίνην καὶ ὕπαγε εἰς τὸν οἶκόν σου
verses.king_james: But that ye may know that the Son of man hath power on earth to forgive sins, (then saith he to the sick of the palsy,) Arise, take up thy bed, and go unto thine house.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et surrexit, et abiit in domum suam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐγερθεὶς ἀπῆλθεν εἰς τὸν οἶκον αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: And he arose, and departed to his house.

verses.latin_vulgate: Videntes autem turbæ timuerunt, et glorificaverunt Deum, qui dedit potestatem talem hominibus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἰδόντες δὲ οἱ ὄχλοι ἐθαύμασαν καὶ ἐδόξασαν τὸν θεὸν τὸν δόντα ἐξουσίαν τοιαύτην τοῖς ἀνθρώποις
verses.king_james: But when the multitudes saw *it*, they marvelled, and glorified God, which had given such power unto men.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et, cum transiret inde Jesus, vidit hominem sedentem in telonio, Matthæum nomine. Et ait illi: Sequere me. Et surgens, secutus est eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ παράγων ὁ ἰησοῦς ἐκεῖθεν εἶδεν ἄνθρωπον καθήμενον ἐπὶ τὸ τελώνιον ματθαῖον λεγόμενον καὶ λέγει αὐτῷ ἀκολούθει μοι καὶ ἀναστὰς ἠκολούθησεν αὐτῷ
verses.king_james: And as Jesus passed forth from thence, he saw a man, named Matthew, sitting at the receipt of custom: and he saith unto him, Follow me. And he arose, and followed him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et factum est, discumbente eo in domo, ecce multi publicani et peccatores venientes, discumbebant cum Jesu, et discipulis ejus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐγένετο αὐτοῦ ἀνακειμένου ἐν τῇ οἰκίᾳ καὶ ἰδού πολλοὶ τελῶναι καὶ ἁμαρτωλοὶ ἐλθόντες συνανέκειντο τῷ ἰησοῦ καὶ τοῖς μαθηταῖς αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: And it came to pass, as Jesus sat at meat in the house, behold, many publicans and sinners came and sat down with him and his disciples.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et videntes pharisæi, dicebant discipulis ejus: Quare cum publicanis et peccatoribus manducat magister vester?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἰδόντες οἱ φαρισαῖοι εἶπον τοῖς μαθηταῖς αὐτοῦ διατί μετὰ τῶν τελωνῶν καὶ ἁμαρτωλῶν ἐσθίει ὁ διδάσκαλος ὑμῶν
verses.king_james: And when the Pharisees saw *it*, they said unto his disciples, Why eateth your Master with publicans and sinners?

verses.latin_vulgate: At Jesus audiens, ait: Non est opus valentibus medicus, sed male habentibus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ ἰησοῦς ἀκούσας εἶπεν αὐτοῖς οὐ χρείαν ἔχουσιν οἱ ἰσχύοντες ἰατροῦ ἀλλ᾽ οἱ κακῶς ἔχοντες
verses.king_james: But when Jesus heard *that*, he said unto them, They that be whole need not a physician, but they that are sick.

verses.latin_vulgate: Euntes autem discite quid est: Misericordiam volo, et non sacrificium. Non enim veni vocare justos, sed peccatores.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: πορευθέντες δὲ μάθετε τί ἐστιν ἕλεον θέλω καὶ οὐ θυσίαν οὐ γὰρ ἦλθον καλέσαι δικαίους ἀλλ᾽ ἁμαρτωλοὺς εἰς μετάνοιαν
verses.king_james: But go ye and learn what *that* meaneth, I will have mercy, and not sacrifice: for I am not come to call the righteous, but sinners to repentance.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tunc accesserunt ad eum discipuli Joannis, dicentes: Quare nos, et pharisæi, jejunamus frequenter: discipuli autem tui non jejunant?
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τότε προσέρχονται αὐτῷ οἱ μαθηταὶ ἰωάννου λέγοντες διατί ἡμεῖς καὶ οἱ φαρισαῖοι νηστεύομεν πολλά οἱ δὲ μαθηταί σου οὐ νηστεύουσιν
verses.king_james: Then came to him the disciples of John, saying, Why do we and the Pharisees fast oft, but thy disciples fast not?

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ait illis Jesus: Numquid possunt filii sponsi lugere, quamdiu cum illis est sponsus? Venient autem dies cum auferetur ab eis sponsus: et tunc jejunabunt.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ εἶπεν αὐτοῖς ὁ ἰησοῦς μὴ δύνανται οἱ υἱοὶ τοῦ νυμφῶνος πενθεῖν ἐφ᾽ ὅσον μετ᾽ αὐτῶν ἐστιν ὁ νυμφίος ἐλεύσονται δὲ ἡμέραι ὅταν ἀπαρθῇ ἀπ᾽ αὐτῶν ὁ νυμφίος καὶ τότε νηστεύσουσιν
verses.king_james: And Jesus said unto them, Can the children of the bridechamber mourn, as long as the bridegroom is with them? but the days will come, when the bridegroom shall be taken from them, and then shall they fast.

verses.latin_vulgate: Nemo autem immittit commissuram panni rudis in vestimentum vetus: tollit enim plenitudinem ejus a vestimento, et pejor scissura fit.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐδεὶς δὲ ἐπιβάλλει ἐπίβλημα ῥάκους ἀγνάφου ἐπὶ ἱματίῳ παλαιῷ αἴρει γὰρ τὸ πλήρωμα αὐτοῦ ἀπὸ τοῦ ἱματίου καὶ χεῖρον σχίσμα γίνεται
verses.king_james: No man putteth a piece of new cloth unto an old garment, for that which is put in to fill it up taketh from the garment, and the rent is made worse.

verses.latin_vulgate: Neque mittunt vinum novum in utres veteres: alioquin rumpuntur utres, et vinum effunditur, et utres pereunt. Sed vinum novum in utres novos mittunt: et ambo conservantur.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οὐδὲ βάλλουσιν οἶνον νέον εἰς ἀσκοὺς παλαιούς εἰ δὲ μήγε ῥήγνυνται οἱ ἀσκοί καὶ ὁ οἰνος ἐκχεῖται καὶ οἱ ἀσκοὶ ἀπολοῦνται ἀλλὰ βάλλουσιν οἶνον νέον εἰς ἀσκοὺς καινούς καὶ ἀμφότερα συντηροῦνται
verses.king_james: Neither do men put new wine into old bottles: else the bottles break, and the wine runneth out, and the bottles perish: but they put new wine into new bottles, and both are preserved.

verses.latin_vulgate: Hæc illo loquente ad eos, ecce princeps unus accessit, et adorabat eum, dicens: Domine, filia mea modo defuncta est: sed veni, impone manum tuam super eam, et vivet.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ταῦτα αὐτοῦ λαλοῦντος αὐτοῖς ἰδού ἄρχων ἐλθὼν προσεκύνει αὐτῷ λέγων ὅτι ἡ θυγάτηρ μου ἄρτι ἐτελεύτησεν ἀλλὰ ἐλθὼν ἐπίθες τὴν χεῖρά σου ἐπ᾽ αὐτήν καὶ ζήσεται
verses.king_james: While he spake these things unto them, behold, there came a certain ruler, and worshipped him, saying, My daughter is even now dead: but come and lay thy hand upon her, and she shall live.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et surgens Jesus, sequebatur eum, et discipuli ejus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐγερθεὶς ὁ ἰησοῦς ἠκολούθησεν αὐτῷ καὶ οἱ μαθηταὶ αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: And Jesus arose, and followed him, and *so did* his disciples.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ecce mulier, quæ sanguinis fluxum patiebatur duodecim annis, accessit retro, et tetigit fimbriam vestimenti ejus.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἰδού γυνὴ αἱμοῤῥοοῦσα δώδεκα ἔτη προσελθοῦσα ὄπισθεν ἥψατο τοῦ κρασπέδου τοῦ ἱματίου αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: And, behold, a woman, which was diseased with an issue of blood twelve years, came behind *him*, and touched the hem of his garment:

verses.latin_vulgate: Dicebat enim intra se: Si tetigero tantum vestimentum ejus, salva ero.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἔλεγεν γὰρ ἐν ἑαυτῇ ἐὰν μόνον ἅψωμαι τοῦ ἱματίου αὐτοῦ σωθήσομαι
verses.king_james: For she said within herself, If I may but touch his garment, I shall be whole.

verses.latin_vulgate: At Jesus conversus, et videns eam, dixit: Confide, filia, fides tua te salvam fecit. Et salva facta est mulier ex illa hora.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὁ δὲ ἰησοῦς ἐπιστραφεὶς καὶ ἰδὼν αὐτὴν εἶπεν φάρσει θύγατερ ἡ πίστις σου σέσωκέν σε καὶ ἐσώθη ἡ γυνὴ ἀπὸ τῆς ὥρας ἐκείνης
verses.king_james: But Jesus turned him about, and when he saw her, he said, Daughter, be of good comfort; thy faith hath made thee whole. And the woman was made whole from that hour.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cum venisset Jesus in domum principis, et vidisset tibicines et turbam tumultuantem, dicebat:
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐλθὼν ὁ ἰησοῦς εἰς τὴν οἰκίαν τοῦ ἄρχοντος καὶ ἰδὼν τοὺς αὐλητὰς καὶ τὸν ὄχλον θορυβούμενον
verses.king_james: And when Jesus came into the ruler's house, and saw the minstrels and the people making a noise,

verses.latin_vulgate: Recedite: non est enim mortua puella, sed dormit. Et deridebant eum.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: λέγει αὐτοῖς ἀναχωρεῖτε οὐ γὰρ ἀπέθανεν τὸ κοράσιον ἀλλὰ καθεύδει καὶ κατεγέλων αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: He said unto them, Give place: for the maid is not dead, but sleepeth. And they laughed him to scorn.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et cum ejecta esset turba, intravit: et tenuit manum ejus, et surrexit puella.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ὅτε δὲ ἐξεβλήθη ὁ ὄχλος εἰσελθὼν ἐκράτησεν τῆς χειρὸς αὐτῆς καὶ ἠγέρθη τὸ κοράσιον
verses.king_james: But when the people were put forth, he went in, and took her by the hand, and the maid arose.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et exiit fama hæc in universam terram illam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐξῆλθεν ἡ φήμη αὕτη εἰς ὅλην τὴν γῆν ἐκείνην
verses.king_james: And the fame hereof went abroad into all that land.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et transeunte inde Jesu, secuti sunt eum duo cæci, clamantes, et dicentes: Miserere nostri, fili David.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ παράγοντι ἐκεῖθεν τῷ ἰησοῦ ἠκολούθησαν αὐτῷ δύο τυφλοὶ κράζοντες καὶ λέγοντες ἐλέησον ἡμᾶς υἱὲ δαβίδ
verses.king_james: And when Jesus departed thence, two blind men followed him, crying, and saying, *Thou* Son of David, have mercy on us.

verses.latin_vulgate: Cum autem venisset domum, accesserunt ad eum cæci. Et dicit eis Jesus: Creditis quia hoc possum facere vobis? Dicunt ei: Utique, Domine.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἐλθόντι δὲ εἰς τὴν οἰκίαν προσῆλθον αὐτῷ οἱ τυφλοί καὶ λέγει αὐτοῖς ὁ ἰησοῦς πιστεύετε ὅτι δύναμαι τοῦτο ποιῆσαι λέγουσιν αὐτῷ ναί κύριε
verses.king_james: And when he was come into the house, the blind men came to him: and Jesus saith unto them, Believe ye that I am able to do this? They said unto him, Yea, Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tunc tetigit oculos eorum, dicens: Secundum fidem vestram, fiat vobis.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τότε ἥψατο τῶν ὀφθαλμῶν αὐτῶν λέγων κατὰ τὴν πίστιν ὑμῶν γενηθήτω ὑμῖν
verses.king_james: Then touched he their eyes, saying, According to your faith be it unto you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et aperti sunt oculi eorum: et comminatus est illis Jesus, dicens: Videte ne quis sciat.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἀνεῴχθησαν αὐτῶν οἱ ὀφθαλμοί καὶ ἐνεβριμησατο αὐτοῖς ὁ ἰησοῦς λέγων ὁρᾶτε μηδεὶς γινωσκέτω
verses.king_james: And their eyes were opened; and Jesus straitly charged them, saying, See *that* no man know *it*.

verses.latin_vulgate: Illi autem exeuntes, diffamaverunt eum in tota terra illa.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἱ δὲ ἐξελθόντες διεφήμισαν αὐτὸν ἐν ὅλῃ τῇ γῇ ἐκείνῃ
verses.king_james: But they, when they were departed, spread abroad his fame in all that country.

verses.latin_vulgate: Egressis autem illis, ecce obtulerunt ei hominem mutum, dæmonium habentem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: αὐτῶν δὲ ἐξερχομένων ἰδού προσήνεγκαν αὐτῷ ἄνθρωπον κωφὸν δαιμονιζόμενον
verses.king_james: As they went out, behold, they brought to him a dumb man possessed with a devil.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ejecto dæmonio, locutus est mutus, et miratæ sunt turbæ, dicentes: Numquam apparuit sic in Israël.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ ἐκβληθέντος τοῦ δαιμονίου ἐλάλησεν ὁ κωφός καὶ ἐθαύμασαν οἱ ὄχλοι λέγοντες ὅτι οὐδέποτε ἐφάνη οὕτως ἐν τῷ ἰσραήλ
verses.king_james: And when the devil was cast out, the dumb spake: and the multitudes marvelled, saying, It was never so seen in Israel.

verses.latin_vulgate: Pharisæi autem dicebant: In principe dæmoniorum ejicit dæmones.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: οἱ δὲ φαρισαῖοι ἔλεγον ἐν τῷ ἄρχοντι τῶν δαιμονίων ἐκβάλλει τὰ δαιμόνια
verses.king_james: But the Pharisees said, He casteth out devils through the prince of the devils.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et circuibat Jesus omnes civitates, et castella, docens in synagogis eorum, et prædicans Evangelium regni, et curans omnem languorem, et omnem infirmitatem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: καὶ περιῆγεν ὁ ἰησοῦς τὰς πόλεις πάσας καὶ τὰς κώμας διδάσκων ἐν ταῖς συναγωγαῖς αὐτῶν καὶ κηρύσσων τὸ εὐαγγέλιον τῆς βασιλείας καὶ θεραπεύων πᾶσαν νόσον καὶ πᾶσαν μαλακίαν ἐν τῷ λαῷ
verses.king_james: And Jesus went about all the cities and villages, teaching in their synagogues, and preaching the gospel of the kingdom, and healing every sickness and every disease among the people.

verses.latin_vulgate: Videns autem turbas, misertus est eis: quia erant vexati, et jacentes sicut oves non habentes pastorem.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: ἰδὼν δὲ τοὺς ὄχλους ἐσπλαγχνίσθη περὶ αὐτῶν ὅτι ἦσαν ἐκλελυμένοι καὶ ἐῤῥιμμένοι ὡσεὶ πρόβατα μὴ ἔχοντα ποιμένα
verses.king_james: But when he saw the multitudes, he was moved with compassion on them, because they fainted, and were scattered abroad, as sheep having no shepherd.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tunc dicit discipulis suis: Messis quidem multa, operarii autem pauci.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: τότε λέγει τοῖς μαθηταῖς αὐτοῦ ὁ μὲν θερισμὸς πολύς οἱ δὲ ἐργάται ὀλίγοι
verses.king_james: Then saith he unto his disciples, The harvest truly *is* plenteous, but the labourers *are* few;

verses.latin_vulgate: Rogate ergo Dominum messis, ut mittat operarios in messem suam.
verses.greek_textus_receptus: δεήθητε οὖν τοῦ κυρίου τοῦ θερισμοῦ ὅπως ἐκβάλῃ ἐργάτας εἰς τὸν θερισμὸν αὐτοῦ
verses.king_james: Pray ye therefore the Lord of the harvest, that he will send forth labourers into his harvest.
